Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Jul-14-2015 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Order Compelling Trustee To Provide Information And To Account.
Off Calendar Mar-24-2015 Continued To Aug-18-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PTR-11-295125 The Alice E. Barth Trust and PTR-11-295126 The Mariette R. Wilfert Trust.
Appearances: Dominic Campisi for (trustee) Thomas Barth (present in audience). Melissa Karlsten for (beneficiary) Mary Ojakian. Betsy Marr is also present in the audience.

The court stated that there remains significant issues on how to deal with one of the pieces of property and asked if the parties needed a continuance or if they will be able to work it out. Mr. Campisi reported that they have talked about resolution, that they have agreed to go to mediation with Judge James, anticipate dates for mediation in the next month or so, that the property work is moving along, that they have resolved 6 of the 7 encroachments, that they are waiting for a new survey regarding the lot lines, that they have resolved the parcel issue and it is now certified and that they hope to resolve the issue of fees with Judge James. Ms. Karlsten stated that she is in agreement with mediation, is hopeful that they will resolve the issues of the Deep Woods property and fees and requested a continuance to August. The court agreed to continue the petitions for order compelling trustee to provide information and to account (in both cases)to August 18, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 stating that it wants this resolved and that the reasons for the petitions no longer exist; the property is not part of the pending petition.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Compelling Trustee To Provide Information And To Account.
Off Calendar Mar-10-2015 Continued To Mar-24-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Compelling Trustee To Provide Information And To Account.
Off Calendar Feb-10-2015 Continued To Mar-10-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Compelling Trustee To Provide Information And To Account.
Off Calendar Jan-06-2015 Continued To Feb-10-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PTR-11-295125 The Alice E. Barth Trust and PTR-11-295126 The Mariette R. Wilfert Trust.

Appearances: Dominic Campisi for Thomas Barth (Trustee).

Mr. Campisi stated that the petitions really are moot, that the Trustee has given his Sisters income and exchange information, that they gave Ms. Karlsten formal accountings and that they have passed out all of the properties. Mr. Campisi also stated that only the big rural property is remaining, that there is an encroachment issue which is in litigation, that there are title issues and a variety of other problems, and that the only issue is the attorney's fees. The Court questioned why the petitioner was not present, stated that it will continue the matter for one last time for the Parties to specify what if any issues remain in these pending petitions and whether these petitions are moot and can go off calendar. The Court also stated that if the Parties fail to file anything or show up, the petitions will go off calendar. Both Petitions for Order Compelling Trustee to Provide Information and to Account were continued to February 10, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy S. Park. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Compelling Trustee To Provide Information And To Account.
Off Calendar Nov-05-2013 Continued To Mar-25-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following cases were heard: PTR-11-295125 The Alice Barth Trust and PTR-11-295126 The Mariette Wilfert Trust.

Appearances: Dominic Campisi for and with Thomas Barth (Trustee). David Hines for and with Elizabeth Mar (Beneficiary). Melissa Karlsten and Matthew Owens for and with Mary Ojakian (Beneficiary/Petitioner).

Mr. Campisi stated that the remaining issues are Attorney fees, Trustee fees and the deep woods property, that they now have a survey of the property but that he would like an agreement as to confidentiality, and that they hired a zoning firm. Ms. Karlsten stated that her Client would like to see the property distributed or sold, that her Client has no interest in developing the property prior to being sold and that the next step is to set a trial out far enough out so they could go to mediation. The Petition for Order Compelling Trustee to Provide Information and to Account was continued to March 25, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of the deep woods property, updating fee requests, mediation, trial setting and a 2013 accounting.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Compelling Trustee To Provide Information And To Account.
Off Calendar Jun-11-2013 Continued To Sep-24-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PTR-11-295125 The Alice Barth Trust and PTR-11-295126 The Mariette Wilfert Trust.

Appearances: Dominic Campisi for Thomas Barth. Brian Kulich for Mary Ojakian. David Hines for Elizabeth Mars.

Mr. Campisi stated that they got all of the property distributed, that they are dealing with the Board of Equalization with regard to escape assessments and that only the Santa Cruz property remains. In addition, Mr. Campisi stated that the Surveyor developed maps, that the Surveyor will attempt to legalize 3 parcels and that they engaged the services of Attorney Charlene Addick to deal with the encroachment issue to assist in providing access to the properties. Mr. Kulich requested 90-days to resolve issues with the accounting. The Petition for Order Compelling Trustee to Provide Information and to Account was continued to September 24, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

Both Petitions for Order Compelling Trustee to Provide Information and to Account were continued to June 11, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to see where the Parties are.

Mr. Campisi stated that they have been provided with a new appraisal on the Ralston property, that the Parties do not want to put the Safeway parking lot in for trade, that they are willing to put the Natoma and the Minna properties in for trade, that the environmental issue with the Santa Cruz property is still pending, that they are making progress and that written agreement has been circulated but that some changes have to be made. Counsel were directed to discuss the issues prior to the matter being called. Upon returning, Mr. Campisi stated that they were trying to work on a number of issues which have tax components, that they will work their way through to minimize expenses, that there were a number of similar properties that are not part of the Trusts, that the property in the Santa Cruz mountains has environmental issues and that they hope to resolve the matter. Both Petitions for Order Compelling Trustee to Provide Information and to Account were continued to July 17, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for further status.
